Title: A fungal sesquiterpene biosynthesis gene cluster critical for mutualist-pathogen transition in Colletotrichum tofieldiae

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: ABA branch of the ABA-BOT secondary metabolite cluster
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: putatively catalyzes an oxidative step in fungal ABA biosynthesis downstream of farnesyl diphosphate cyclization
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms intermediate precursor for target product
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: activates host ABA signaling and contributes to fungal virulence/pathogenesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: strongly induced during early root colonization/pathogenesis at 10 dpi; reduced at 24 dpi; suppressed at 26°C
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: low Pi versus normal Pi; root colonization; elevated temperature (26°C) suppresses cluster expression broadly
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: required for full activation of host ABA responses, fungal root colonization, sucrose accumulation in roots, and Ct3-mediated plant growth inhibition
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: ABA and related ABA intermediates were not detected under tested culture conditions; gene function is inferred from homology to BcABA2 and infection phenotypes
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: targeted knockout; RT-qPCR of host ABA marker AtMAPKKK18; fungal biomass assay; plant growth assay; sucrose measurement; comparative transcriptomics
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: dual RNA-seq (DDBJ: DRA012868, DRA012867); fungal genome assembly (DDBJ/NCBI: BQXV01)
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: supports pathogenicity and host growth inhibition rather than beneficial interaction
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: cluster disruption could reduce fungal virulence and convert interaction toward plant growth promotion under low Pi
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Ct3ABA2 is an early infection-induced ABA-cluster gene in pathogenic Ct3. 2. Ct3ABA2 knockout reduces host ABA-marker induction, fungal root biomass, root sucrose accumulation, and plant growth inhibition. 3. Ct3ABA2 is therefore required for Ct3 pathogenic lifestyle transition via the fungal ABA-BOT cluster.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: committed initial cyclization step of fungal ABA branch
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: putatively catalyzes the initial cyclization of farnesyl diphosphate to the sesquiterpene scaffold used for fungal ABA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies committed precursor for target product
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: activates host ABA pathway and promotes fungal virulence
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: highly induced during early root infection at 10 dpi; low/undetectable at 24 dpi; expression strongly reduced at 26°C
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: infection stage; phosphate status; elevated temperature
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: required for Ct3 pathogenesis, host ABA response activation, and efficient root colonization; loss alleviates pathogenicity
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: homologous to BcABA3, which catalyzes initial step of fungal ABA biosynthesis; exogenous ABA restores virulence of mutant; mutant can promote plant growth under high temperature
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: targeted knockout; complementation-related physiological rescue by exogenous ABA; RT-qPCR; fungal biomass assay; plant growth assay; infection-temperature expression assay
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: committed-step enzyme (inferred from pathway position), but rate-limiting not established
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: dual RNA-seq (DDBJ: DRA012868, DRA012867); fungal genome assembly (BQXV01)
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: promotes pathogenic lifestyle and host growth inhibition
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: candidate antifungal target; disabling this step suppresses virulence and can favor mutualistic behavior under some conditions
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Ct3ABA3 is an early infection-induced sesquiterpene synthase-like ABA gene in the Ct3 ABA-BOT cluster. 2. Disruption decreases host ABA-marker induction, fungal biomass, and plant growth inhibition. 3. High temperature suppresses Ct3ABA3 expression, correlating with pathogen-to-mutualist transition.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: putatively catalyzes an oxidative tailoring step in BOT biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms intermediate precursor for target product
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: fungal culture; likely infection sites in roots
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: contributes to host ABA response activation and fungal virulence
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: required for full Ct3-mediated induction of host ABA marker and plant growth inhibition
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: annotated P450 in monophyletic ABA/BOT P450 clade; knockout tested for virulence but metabolite step not directly assayed
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: targeted knockout; host gene expression assay; plant growth phenotype assay
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: dual RNA-seq (DDBJ: DRA012868, DRA012867); genome assembly (BQXV01)
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: possible antifungal engineering target within fungal virulence cluster
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Ct3BOT1 is a co-induced BOT-cluster P450 during pathogenic root colonization. 2. Its disruption reduces Ct3 virulence-associated host ABA marker induction. 3. This supports a role for BOT biosynthesis in pathogenic lifestyle establishment.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: putatively catalyzes an oxidative tailoring step in BOT biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms intermediate precursor for target product
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: fungal culture; likely infection sites in roots
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: required for full induction of host ABA marker and fungal pathogenicity
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: functional assignment supported by cluster conservation and knockout phenotype rather than direct metabolite identification
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: targeted knockout; host marker analysis; plant growth phenotype assay
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Ct3BOT3 is a pathogen-associated BOT cluster gene induced early during infection. 2. Knockout compromises Ct3 virulence-related host ABA response. 3. BOT-cluster function is therefore linked to pathogenic lifestyle.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: required for biosynthesis of BOT intermediates 4β-acetoxyprobotryan-9β-ol and 4β-acetoxyprobotryane-9β,15α-diol; exact reaction not resolved in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: intermediate(s) leading to botrydial pathway metabolites
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for production of target-pathway intermediates and virulence-associated BOT branch output
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: fungal culture on rice medium; function manifests during Arabidopsis root infection
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: promotes host ABA signaling activation, root colonization, sucrose accumulation, and pathogenicity
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: strongly induced at 10 dpi during pathogenesis; low in beneficial Ct4; reduced at 26°C
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: rice medium reveals intermediates; infection stage; low Pi; high temperature suppresses expression
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: essential for BOT intermediate accumulation and for Ct3 pathogenic lifestyle; knockout abolishes plant growth inhibition and instead promotes plant growth under low Pi
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: complementation with Ct3BOT5 restores mutant phenotype; strong evidence that BOT branch is decisive for pathogen-to-mutualist switch
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: targeted knockout; metabolite profiling by GC-MS; complementation; RT-qPCR; fungal biomass assay; plant growth assays
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control point for BOT-dependent pathogenicity, but enzymatic rate limitation not formally established
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: dual RNA-seq (DRA012868, DRA012867); fungal genome assembly (BQXV01)
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: loss of gene switches fungus from pathogenic to beneficial under low Pi
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: high-value target for disabling fungal virulence; manipulation could convert pathogen-like interaction into growth-promoting interaction
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Ct3BOT5 is strongly induced during pathogenic early root infection but repressed in beneficial contexts and at high temperature. 2. Ct3BOT5 is required for accumulation of detected BOT intermediates in culture. 3. Ct3BOT5 knockout reduces host ABA response and fungal biomass and converts Ct3 from pathogenic to beneficial under low Pi; complementation restores pathogenicity.

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: secondary metabolite biosynthesis in the BOT branch, likely coordinated ABA-BOT cluster expression
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: BOT cluster genes (BOT1-BOT5, BOT7) and possibly linked ABA-BOT cluster genes
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: likely activates co-expressed BOT biosynthetic genes during pathogenesis
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: not directly tested functionally, but strong expression with BOT genes suggests cluster-level regulator role
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: root infection stage; environmental conditions such as temperature likely influence overall cluster expression
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: early root colonization; phosphate status; high temperature suppresses overall cluster expression
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: supports fungal virulence and host manipulation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: associated with expression of pathogenic ABA-BOT cluster during Ct3 infection
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: core gene status is based on explicit strong pathogenesis-associated expression within the cluster; no knockout provided
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: dual RNA-seq co-expression evidence; comparative genomics
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: potential future target for blocking cluster activation, though unvalidated here
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Ct3BOT6 encodes a Zn2Cys6 transcription-factor-like protein within the BOT cluster. 2. It is strongly expressed during pathogenic Ct3 infection together with biosynthetic genes. 3. The paper suggests regulatory coupling within the cluster, although direct functional validation is not shown.
